# Backend Systems Brief

The `load-detail` operation is owned by the backend item service. It accepts the selected public item identifier and returns either the public detail payload or a classified failure that the interface can translate into `detail-error`. No account identity, payment state, private record, favorite, or cross-device preference is required for this first version.

The frontend owns request initiation, content-shaped loading feedback, retry focus behavior, and preserving the selected item while a retry is in progress. The backend owns lookup consistency and the distinction between retryable service failure and unavailable item. Refresh and back behavior must not depend on hidden session state.

This brief constrains product behavior rather than selecting an API, database, framework, or deployment topology. Those implementation decisions begin only after the accepted product/design handoff.
